title: bridge-matrix: AS transaction body limit is 64 MiB — unbounded per-event allocation
description: bridge-matrix/src/bridge.rs run() sets DefaultBodyLimit::max(64 * 1024 * 1024) on the axum router. The axum handler as_transaction() deserializes the entire body into AsTransaction { events: Vec<MatrixEvent> } before any per-event filtering. A rogue homeserver (or misconfigured federation peer) can send a single 64 MiB transaction containing thousands of MatrixEvent objects. Each event.content is a serde_json::Value, meaning all the JSON tree nodes are heap-allocated at once before the room_id filter runs. This can cause a large transient allocation spike. The body limit prevents the worst case but 64 MiB of JSON events is still a lot of heap churn. This is a P1 operational risk, not a crash.
close_reason: Body limit reduced to 1 MiB; eliminates unbounded 64 MiB allocation spike
